rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY EDA DATI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The petition requests distribution to the estate of Michael Reichling. A personal representative must be named. Please explain who is the personal representative for the estate of Michael Reichling and file a copy of certified letters. See LRSF 14.35 G. If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. 2) 15 days' required notice must be given to the personal representative of the estate of Michael Reichling. 3) Please file ordinary statutory timesheets so that the Court may determine whether extraordinary fees are "just and reasonable" (Prob. C. 10801(a); CRC 7.703(a)). 4) The extraordinary timesheets need to be re-done and must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY EILEEN GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Four of the heirs listed in the omnibus clause of the order of final distribution in the Estate of Anna Griffin are alleged to be deceased with no successors in interest. Petitioner alleges that, after a diligent search, she knows of no personal representatives that have been appointed for these persons. Court to determine whether additional search should be required. 2) No effort was made to market the real property of the estate, and it was sold directly to the creditor, HGFC. Petitioner alleges that no efforts were made because it was worthless due to the fractional interest, and because it could not be built on. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANNE S. OUTIN REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST UNDER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/18/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Mabel Cocking's estate was not formally probated in the UK because National Savings & Investments was apparently her only asset, and those funds were released without the institution requiring a Grant. How is May Findlay's letter dated 22 December 2021 evidence that no formal probate is necessary for the ultimate distribution of these additional funds that Mabel Cocking's estate will likely be receiving? Has the successor trustee's attorney contacted a UK solicitor to confirm that no formal proceeding is still required? Under what authority could this Court distribute this estate to an unappointed executor or to a beneficiary of a purported will that has not been admitted? The accounting still has not been supplemented with an informational schedule showing the remaining escheated assets that were listed on Schedule D-1 of the prior accounting (Merrill Lynch accounts). After these issues are addressed in a SUPPLEMENT to the petition, signed and verified by the successor trustee, an appearance likely will be required in connection with the fees not being sought "at this time" - or ever, since the successor trustee's appointment in 2018 and throughout her subsequent filing of six prior accounting petitions - such that the trust beneficiaries have no idea if/when fees might be sought and how much they might be. Furthermore, if fees had been sought and paid, the amount of bond could have been reduced. (Issues noted by an Examiner in formal Notes or posted in the tentative rulings are not part of the filed record. Any paper addressing issues should stand on its own and not be an "answer" sheet to such issues. For example, paragraph 5 of the "Verified Declaration," etc., filed on 12/16/21 does not make any sense. Finally, the legislature has apparently deemed only two appropriate pleadings either to provide additional information - in a SUPPLEMENT - or to modify the petition - in an AMENDMENT or a fully AMENDED petition. [CRC 7.3.] Please avoid "Declarations" to supplement or amend a petition.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/7/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

That out of the property in executor's hands, the executor is authorized and directed to pay the following: a. To Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. $2,455.01 as statutory executor fees. b. To Harry B. Maring, Attorney at Law $2,455.01 as statutory fees for services to petitioner and to the estate and reimbursement of out of pocket expenses of $750.00 for a total of $3,205.01. This final balance of $63,554.96 in wells fargo bank account no xxx-0800 in hereby ordeded to be paid to Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. as trustee of Robert F. Cowan 1992 Trust, created on July 9, 1992 as amended.

Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. Court to determine how to proceed at hearing. It appears the parties have settled. No petition for approval filed. Court to determine whether the matters should be taken off calendar.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SONOMA COUNTY***IN THE MATTER OF THE REVOCABLE TRUST AGREEMENT OF MARIE J. PALETTA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Recovery Of Trust Property Held By Former Trustee; For Double Damages; For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Still no proposed order. The petition is incomplete. A copy of the trust instrument is not attached. All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. If there was a prior order issued by this Court, this petition should have been filed in that same earlier case. The petition does not indicate the date of that prior order or the existing case number or even attach a copy of that order that purportedly removed Carol Kunkle and appointed Petitioner as the successor trustee. (A search of the Court's register of actions using both this settlor's name or Carol Kunkle's name turned up just one other prior similar case against Respondent but not this trust. Finally, a search by this petitioner's name turned up PTR-18-302145.) An ex parte petition must be filed to consolidate this case with the earlier one, with the earlier one surviving. After this case is consolidated with the exiting case, and a supplement to the petition is then filed in the earlier case, notice of the supplement must also be served. [CRC 7.53(b).] If the cases cannot be consolidated in time for the supplement to be filed in the earlier case at least 30 days before 4/19/22, Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner to request a further continuance. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/8/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PATRICK W. MULKEEN AND ROBIN EICKMAN 1998 TRUST EXECUTED ON SEPTEMBER 11, 1998
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Title To Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/18/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Notice must be given on the mandatory judicial council form DE-115. 2) Examiner received a double sided copy of the filings and the matter will be reviewed when the required endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copy is received. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) Please explain the legal basis for San Francisco being the proper venue. 4) Please state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficaries. (See CRC 7.902). 5) Please file the current deed for the real property. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/4/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIDA QUEBRAL ORQUIZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/30/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities or Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License has been filed. [LRSF 14.18 E.] Item 3e(1) is still checked as grounds for waiving bond, but the decedent died INTESTATE (without a will). All heirs must waive bond or bond will be required. Item 3g(2)(a) should have been checked if the person(s) having actual priority declined to serve and in Attachment 3g(2)(a), the declination of the person(s) having priority (siblings before a niece) must be attached. What is the RELATIONSHIP to the decedent of Rhena Orquiza Morales and Adeluisa Orquiza Cruz? Notice was mailed to Adeluisa Orquiza Cruz at an address other than the one shown in item 8 of the petition. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] Whose children are the nieces and nephews? Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? It does not seem likely that Ely Sonny F. Orquiza was the decedent's father, as stated on the Judicial Council form "Declaration" filed on 2/14/22. A Judicial Council form Declaration must not be used for a supplement to a pleading. [CRC 7.104(b).] (The proof of service filed on 2/14/22 did not show on the face of the Notice that an AMENDED petition had been filed; however, since the published notice showed that an amended petition was filed, the Court might waive this defect at the hearing when this amended petition is ready to be heard.) Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WAYNE ANDERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Warren Anderson is presumably the parent of the niece and nephew. When did he die? When did Catherine Van Zee die? Item 7 of the petition must be answered if the decedent was not survived by spouse or issue. A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Waiver of Bond is a mandatory Judicial Council form. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. The proof of publication filed on 2/22/22 shows the petitioner as the Special Administrator...? (This Court no longer requires the Declaration of Real Property.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (such as a supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/4/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
